title: Here's A Nut
content:
"Here's a nut, there's a nut;
        Hide it quick away,
        In a hole, under leaves,
        To eat some winter day.
        Acorns sweet are plenty,
        We will have them all:
        Skip and scamper lively
        Till the last ones fall."
meaning:
This evocative piece by Louisa May Alcott, titled "Here's A Nut", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
